## Summary

Addresses the next top-down item from the deficiency roadmap: replacing `/resources` with a real Resources Hub.

## Why

The roadmap calls out `/resources` as needing to become the intellectual trust center of the site, with cards for Black Box, Methodology, Reliability, Author FAQ, Privacy & Research Controls, Genre FAQ, and Storygate FAQ.

## Changes

- Reframes `/resources` as the trust center for serious manuscript evaluation.
- Expands resource cards to include:
  - The Black Box Problem
  - Methodology
  - Reliability / Editorial Doctrine
  - Author FAQ
  - Privacy & Research Controls
  - Genre & Classification FAQ
  - Storygate Studio FAQ
- Adds a clear evaluation-depth section:
  - Short-form evaluation: under 25,000 words, 13 criteria only
  - Long-form evaluation: 25,000+ words, manuscript-scale behavior
  - Long-form multi-layer evaluation: deeper architecture review for complex manuscripts
- Rebuilds the Author FAQ section around current manuscript-only scope.
- Adds an initial Privacy & Research Controls trust section.
- Adds Genre & Classification FAQ content.
- Adds Storygate Studio FAQ content with manuscript-only, publishing-facing scope.

## Scope

Public `/resources` page copy and structure only.

No backend, database, auth, evaluation, report, pricing, Storygate runtime, Agent Readiness runtime, or pipeline behavior changed.

## Follow-up

This does not yet create dedicated standalone pages for Privacy & Research Controls, Genre FAQ, or Storygate FAQ. It creates the hub structure and anchor sections first, so the next PR can promote the privacy/security material into a dedicated page.
